2) I might find some journal prompts to answer during my notes in the evenings to see if I can find any more emotional triggers...
3) I wish I had put more emotional entries this week since this week had a lot of workload...

Rena Nguyen (week 2 reflection) | I continue to watch one movie per day to fit with my schedule. I proceeded to watch a mix of new movies and movies that i already have watched as i mentioned in the first reflection. This week consisted of mostly animated movies, with one movie being non-animated. Out of all the movies, only two had a motivation score above five. While i consider animated movies to be my favorite movie genre, perhaps rewatching movies (maybe those that i like?) might provoke a higher motivation score because i am willing to put in more effort to talk about how much i like it. I managed to complete only one writing project of one movie out of the two movies within the time that i had to myself. For this particular project, i accumulated a word count of 490 words and a flesch reading ease score of 63.4. While i barely missed the mark for hitting the 500 word count that i set for myself, the reading score indicates that my writing is standard and easy to read for the average adult. I will continue to strive for a higher word count while maintaining a similar reading score throughout future writing projects.

JESSICA BAEK (WEEK 4 REFLECTION)
Emotion Label (y-axis): Pleasant, Unpleasant
X-axis values: 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25
Legend: High (blue), Low (olive/dark yellow)
In this project, I tracked my emotions, energy levels, triggers, and journaling habits over 28 days. I wanted to understand how emotional awareness changes based on journaling and identifying triggers. For this plot, the graph shows how often I experienced pleasant vs unpleasant emotions. The graph shows that pleasant emotions occurred more frequently than unpleasant ones. This suggests that overall, my emotional state was more positive during the tracking period. This supports my hypothesis that tracking emotions help improve emotional awareness, since I was able to recognize more positive patterns.

Title: JESSICA BAEK (WEEK 4 REFLECTION)

Caption: This graph shows how pleasant high-energy emotions and unpleasant low-energy emotions changed over time. The pleasant high-energy emotions increased more quickly than unpleasant ones. This means that over time, I experienced more positive emotional states. This supports the idea that frequent mood journaling and check-ins improve emotional awareness and emotional outcomes.

Hypothesis #1 - watching movies lead toward increased development in writing Based on the combined data of both charts, it can be determined that the results somewhat confirm my hypothesis. Taking away the days where no writing was done, the word counts of each project ranges from a word count of 490 to 579. I originally set the goal of each project to have a word count of at least 500 words and most projects have exceeded it. On the other hand, the reading score remained the same throughout each project, with a score of 9. The original goal was to get a score of 8-9. While each project scored the same, i would’ve wished for some projects to fluctuate to an 8.

Hypothesis #2 - watching movies can lead towards increased motivation in writing The results confirm my hypothesis. The chart fluctuates between high and low motivation scores, with the lowest being 1 and the highest being an 8.5. At the beginning of this project, scores remained low as i watched movies that did not give me a spark to write. However, once i managed to watch the movies that did, then it causes the scores to uptick, thus its gradual increase towards the end of the project.

What is the distribution of movie genres? Across all movies that i watched, i tracked a total of 7 different genres. These genres included: action, animation, comedy, drama, family, fantasy, and romance. Out of the 7, animation held the highest count with 8 movies being tracked. Following in with a tie is comedy and drama with a respective 5. Our lowest movie genre that was tracked was family, with a total of 2 movies.

What is the distribution of movie genres? Across all movies that i watched, i tracked a total of 7 different genres. These genres included: action, animation, comedy, drama, family, fantasy, and romance. Out of the 7, animation held the highest count with 8 movies being tracked. Following in with a tie is comedy and drama with a respective 5. Our lowest movie genre that was tracked was family, with a total of 2 movies.

What is the relationship between movie genres and motivation? I don’t think the results show if there is a relationship between movie genres and my motivation scores. Take for example the fantasy scores, while one movie has a score of an 8, the other two scored a 2 and a 1. Movie genres do not singlehandedly play a role in my motivation scores, as outside factors also contribute. While i tend to gravitate towards animation movies, it can be seen that some animated movie are scored low. Overall, it can be said that there is no relationship between the two. Movie genres alone cannot dictate my motivation to write something. What matters is if i have it in me to actually write and the content inside the movie.

Throughout this journey, i’ve come to learn that this project was a boost in helping me do something that i’ve been stuck on trying to do since last year. If it weren’t for this project, i wouldn’t have been motivated to write more, especially if it was just about how much i liked a movie. This project opened my eyes to how i come to write, just in general and when motivated because a movie made me do so. Of course while doing this project, i came across barriers. While i wished to have watched more movies, i was restrained to watching just one everyday. But despite that constraint, it actually allowed me to digest movies more rather than watching movies back to back. The results to my project isn’t perfect as i have came across struggles from my busy schedule. Even though this was a month-ish long project, i was at least able to grow in some way, in both my writing and how i look at movies. As i conclude my reflection, i now look at the 3 unfinished or unstarted drafts from the movie that inspired me to revolve my project around writing about movies. I hope that when i have time, i am able to fully put my knowledge that i have gained from this project into it.
